title: ricinocarpus zollingeri
intro: Ricinocarpus zollingeri is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the Tetracarpidium conophorum in some regions, and is native to Western Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain ricin, a toxic compound, and ricinoleic acid, an unsaturated fatty acid with potential health benefits. The health benefits of Ricinocarpus zollingeri seeds include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of various infections. Traditional herbal preparations include crushing the seeds to make a paste or oil, which can be applied topically to treat skin conditions and wounds, or taken orally in small amounts under medical supervision.
